notes: Problem templates. The engine instantiates these per region according to that region's problem_palette_weights and the current escalation clock. Each template has a problem_type (matched against agent specialty_problem_types), a base_severity, a tick_per_day (how fast it compounds untreated), an effort_to_resolve (agent-days needed), and a per-template list of effects that fire at thresholds.

templates.title: Memorial Grief
templates.flavor: The Sinkhole anniversary is in mid-July; the riverboat anniversary is in late August. The grief in Graustark is a thing the nodes carry. Untreated, it bleeds into family_succession problems.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: Grief metastasised into the storefront's succession. The son's posts grow shorter.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Someone the family already trusts — or someone who's earned the right to be there without asking. A demon sent to a grief problem will resolve it faster but the grief will be heavier on them when they return.

This is a multi-stage mission — your agent will check in across three afternoons. Each check-in is a choice.

stages.title: the cemetery, Tuesday afternoon
stages.body:
{agent} parked at the gate at 2:42 PM. Sammy is at Pop's marker. He has been there since 1:18. He is holding the flowers Mom cut from the back garden — still wrapped in the kitchen towel Mom uses for flowers. The towel is the towel Pop liked.

Sammy has not looked up. {agent} can see him from the gate. The gate squeaks. The gate has squeaked since 1972.

choices.label: walk in. don't say anything yet.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · slow, attentive · no cost

choices.label: wait at the gate. let him come to you.
choices.summary: +0.5 effort · respects his timing · slower close

choices.label: call Mom from the car phone first.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · brings the family in · −1 cover (billed line)

effects.reason: the car phone call to Mom went on a billed line

choices.label: run hasslein's grief-visit protocol · announce · sit · forty-five minutes.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the protocol carries the visit · requires reading BP_016 (hasslein's protocol post)

stages.title: the sit-with
stages.body:
Sammy is now sitting on the grass next to the marker. {agent} is two paces back. The wind is from the southwest. The flowers are still in the towel.

This is the part that matters. Sammy has not been ready to talk in three Tuesdays. He looks ready today. Or close.

choices.label: sit with him. the whole afternoon if it takes.
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · stays late · canon var locks (third Sunday held)

choices.label: tell him a story about Pop. the boat story from '79.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the boat story · Sammy carries it home heavier (W3 burn)

effects.reason: the boat story landed harder than the agent expected

choices.label: ask him about the new chip on the marker's corner.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · practical · opens a thread about the marker's care

stages.title: the drive home
stages.body:
5:18 PM. The sun is on the back of the truck. Sammy is in the passenger seat. The flowers are at the marker; the towel is folded on the dash.

The storefront is twelve minutes by the river road, eight by Ninth. Mom is at the storefront either way. Sammy hasn't said where he wants to go.

choices.label: take Ninth. Mom is at the storefront and the day is the day.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · directly to Mom · the family closes the loop

choices.label: take the river road. let him watch the water.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · slow road · Sammy carries the day home in his own time

choices.label: ask him where he wants to go.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · gives him the choice · what he says becomes a canon fact

resolution_flavor_success: {agent} sat the third Sunday with Sammy. Pop's marker got fresh flowers. Mom waved from the gate at five.
The candles at the riverboat memorial were lit by hands that knew the order. The wax pooled correctly. The grief did not metastasise this week.
{agent} brought the right silence to the right room. T. did not have to say thank you. T. did not have to say anything.
The casserole made it to the right house. The right house was not the house the casserole's note said. {agent} knew which house was the right house.
resolution_flavor_failure: {agent} brought the wrong flowers. Mom kept them anyway. Mom always keeps them. The grief is heavier because Mom is the one who's keeping.
The cemetery was empty Tuesday. Sammy did not come. {agent} did not wait. Sammy came at five and the cemetery was empty for him too.
The right silence was not at the right room when {agent} got there. The room is the room it was last summer. Worse.

templates.title: Family Succession
templates.flavor: Who runs what after Dante. The storefront's articles of incorporation. The boat's memorial fund. The cathedral's lease. Untreated, the storefront loses its insurance renewal.

if_unresolved_at_severity_8.text: Insurance lapsed at the storefront. Closed for three weeks while it sorts out.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Whoever has standing at the D'Ambrosio table. Sending the wrong person is not a failure — it's a different choice. The family will absorb it.

This is a multi-stage mission — three afternoons at the kitchen table.

stages.title: the storefront's articles of incorporation
stages.body:
{agent} sat at the kitchen table at 2:18 PM. T. brought the file from the storefront's back office in a manila folder labeled INCORP-92 in Mom's handwriting. The articles are typed on a Selectric. Pop's signature is on page three. The signature line for the surviving heir is blank.

T. says the surviving heir is the surviving heir. Mom says the surviving heir is the surviving son. They are saying two different things.

choices.label: sign 'the surviving son' on the line. T.'s name in full.
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · canon: the storefront passes to T. · Mom may carry it heavy

effects.reason: Mom carried the signing home heavier than the signing was

choices.label: sign 'the surviving heir' — leave it open. let next month decide.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · postpones the canon · no immediate cost

choices.label: call WIRE_MOTHER on the kitchen phone before signing.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the matriarch advises · costs 1 cover (long-distance call routed through the storefront line)

effects.reason: long-distance call to Lubbock on the storefront's billed line

stages.title: the boat's memorial fund
stages.body:
The memorial fund's check ledger is on the kitchen table at 4:42 PM. Mom has been writing checks from the fund since 1994 to the families of the boat's crew. The balance is $11,840.62. The annual contribution this October would bring it under $10,000 for the first time since the fund opened.

T. wants to honor October. Mom wants the fund to stay above $10K through Aria's eighteenth birthday in 2000. {agent} is at the table between them.

choices.label: honor October at full · the families will read the envelope.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the families get the canonical contribution · the fund dips under $10K

choices.label: half · pace the giving across two seasons.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · fund preserved · families get less but get more later

choices.label: read the back-of-the-bar thread first.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the surviving son's old posts about the fund · only legible if you've been listening

stages.title: the cathedral's lease
stages.body:
Day three, 11:14 AM. The cathedral office. The lease renewal is on the desk. The parish board needs the storefront to be the storefront when the lease lands on their Tuesday meeting. The storefront's articles + the memorial fund are the two things the parish board reads first.

The lease is yours to sign — but the storefront's standing as an entity is what the parish reads.

choices.label: sign · the storefront stands, the cathedral keeps the lease.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the canonical good outcome · the family + parish + cathedral all hold

choices.label: table it · ask for thirty more days.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · postponement · the parish board notes the delay

effects.reason: the parish board reads the delay as ambivalence

resolution_flavor_success: The articles got signed at the right kitchen table. {agent} brought the right pen. The pen was Pop's.
The succession plan landed in the file cabinet. Hector's boy's name is on the second line in pencil. The pencil will be in pen by Labor Day.
resolution_flavor_failure: The wrong name went on the second line. {agent} did not know the right name. The family will have to undo it Tuesday.

templates.title: Lease and Licensing
templates.flavor: The cathedral's variance. The storefront's beer-and-wine renewal. The model home's certificate of occupancy. The Harmony Creek HOA is doing exactly what the HOA is supposed to do.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Paperwork wants paperwork hands. A demon can do it but the demon walks home heavier. A human can do it but the human is the one whose name goes on the form. Pick the cost.

This is a multi-stage mission — clerk visit, paperwork, signatures.

stages.title: the records-desk visit
stages.body:
{agent} parked at the records desk at 10:18 AM. The clerk at window two is the woman who's been at window two since '88. She knows who you are; she does not look up. The form is the form. The variance / renewal / occupancy / whatever-you-came-for is in the drawer behind her.

How {agent} stands at the counter changes which drawer she opens.

choices.label: ask plainly · the form you came for.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · she opens the right drawer · no cost

choices.label: small-talk first · ask about her grandkids.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the clerk sets the form aside for tomorrow · no cost

choices.label: drop a name · invoke the cathedral lease.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · she opens the drawer faster · -1 cover (the name moves through the office gossip-by-noon)

effects.reason: name dropped at the records desk; the office gossip carries it

stages.title: whose name goes on the form
stages.body:
The form is on the desk. Three signature lines. One is the agent's; one is the supervisor's (the supervisor signs off the day's variances at lunch); the third is the witness.

The witness can be the clerk, the agent's own emergency contact, or someone else from the office. Each choice routes the form differently.

choices.label: let the clerk witness · simple, in-house.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · stays in the building · the variance is filed by 5 PM

choices.label: your own emergency contact · keeps it private.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · slower · the office gossip stays out

choices.label: ask the_rectory_aide to come down · sets up the church-parish goodwill.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · costs nothing now · the aide will note the favor

resolution_flavor_success: The variance got stamped. The clerk was the right clerk. The form went in the right pile, which is the pile the clerk does on Tuesday.
{agent} pulled the file at lunch. The file was there. The renewal was 90 seconds of signatures and the seal had ink.
resolution_flavor_failure: {agent} got the wrong clerk. The wrong clerk does the right pile but only on Thursday. The form will be Thursday's problem.

templates.title: Infrastructure Failing
templates.flavor: The diner's walk-in. The cathedral's HVAC. The storefront's grease trap. The model home's foundation crack. Things break in old buildings and in new ones.

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.text: The infrastructure failed in a way that's now visible. The node is dark for the week.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Hands. Someone who's worked the building or worked one like it. Send a fast hand and the fix is sloppy; send a careful hand and the fix is slow.

This is a multi-stage mission — the diagnosis, the fix, the follow-up.

stages.title: the diagnosis · what actually broke
stages.body:
{agent} is at the failing thing at 10:42 AM. The presenting symptom is the symptom — the boiler kicks off, the walk-in cycles wrong, the panel trips at noon. The actual cause is not the symptom.

Three paths to diagnose. Each path leads to a different fix in stage 2.

choices.label: trace it. cable by cable, valve by valve.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · careful · finds the real cause · takes the day

choices.label: replace the obvious thing and call it good.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · fast · may have fixed the symptom not the cause · queues a 4-week burn

effects.reason: the obvious thing was the symptom, not the cause

choices.label: read the workshop thread on the cathedral AC swap.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the canon technical knowledge · only legible if you've read THE_WORKSHOP

stages.title: the fix · with what you have on the truck
stages.body:
Whatever {agent} brought is what's on the truck. The parts in the toolbox are the parts in the toolbox. The right part is at the supply shop in Mobile and the supply shop in Mobile is closed until Tuesday.

Do it now with what's here, or wait until Tuesday.

choices.label: now · with the part on the truck. it'll hold.
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · resolves the problem · slight chance of a re-fail later

choices.label: wait until Tuesday · right part, right install.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · postponed fix · the problem ages a week

choices.label: improvise with the matriarch's binding-code wisdom.
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the right work the wrong way · costs nothing now but JF will note

resolution_flavor_success: {agent} found the loose connection at 4:18 PM. The fix took 22 minutes. The boiler is humming the right semitone again.
The conduit work {agent} did in '91 was the conduit work that needed checking. They checked it. It held.
resolution_flavor_failure: {agent} fixed the wrong thing. The wrong thing was a real thing that needed fixing. The right thing is still wrong. Two visits Saturday.

templates.title: Cathedral Visitor
templates.flavor: Someone is in the warehouse-cathedral who shouldn't be. Most of the time it's a kid who walked in through the back; sometimes it's somebody worse.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Quiet eyes. Someone who can read a room without being read. A demon assigned to a watch can read it deeper but if they're spotted the cover damage is real.

This is a multi-stage mission — the watch, the read, the decision.

stages.title: the watch · 8 to 11 in the back pew
stages.body:
{agent} took the last pew at 7:48 PM. The 8 AM rehearsal is in the choir loft. The visitor — the person who shouldn't be in the warehouse-cathedral — is at the chapel of repose, just out of sight from the back pew. Three hours of watching.

The visitor is doing the visitor's thing. Whatever that is, it's quiet.

choices.label: watch from the pew. note who they look at.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · careful surveillance · low risk of being read

choices.label: walk past the chapel of repose at 9:42 PM · close read.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · gets the canon read · slight cover risk

effects.reason: agent's close-read pass past the visitor

stages.title: the read · who they are, what they came for
stages.body: By midnight {agent} has the read. The visitor came for one of three reasons. The reason determines the response. There is no fourth reason.

choices.label: a kid · they came in through the back · feed them, send them home.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · clean resolution · sets a flag the cathedral notes · unlocks Magician · blow_out_the_candles

choices.label: a journalist · politely indicate they should leave.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · resolution + cover cost · they won't be back tonight

effects.reason: the journalist will write down how they were asked to leave

choices.label: someone else's agent · they were sent here on purpose.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · this is the worst read · queues a follow-up · sets a watch flag

effects.reason: the agent reports back; the enemy now knows what time the chapel is empty

choices.label: ask the river-house thread first · is this on their schedule?
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the hidden-board intel · only legible if you've dialled the river house

resolution_flavor_success: {agent} sat in the last pew from 8 to 11. The visitor came at 9:42. {agent} knew the visitor's father. The visitor left with a cup of coffee and a name to call.
It was a kid. It is always a kid. {agent} gave the kid a sandwich and the bus number that goes back to the kid's grandmother.
resolution_flavor_failure: {agent} was made. The visitor was not a visitor. The visitor was a reporter. The cathedral keeps a record now.

templates.title: Diner Threshold
templates.flavor: Someone is sitting at booth six who shouldn't be. The threshold the diner maintains has had a soft spot all summer.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Booth 6 is not negotiated · it's noticed. The first pass is reading the room from the wear-spot · the second is whether you sit across from him or send the right server. This is a two-stage mission.

stages.title: the read from the wear-spot
stages.body:
{agent} walked the diner counter-to-back at 2:11 PM and clocked Booth 6 from the wear-spot — the linoleum patch by the ticket wheel where the regulars used to stand. The man in Booth 6 is the wrong-shaped silhouette for that booth. Coffee untouched. The fluorescent over the booth is the older one that flickers on the half-second. He's reading it.

What {agent} does with the first pass changes who he reads back.

choices.label: keep moving · log it · don't engage.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · clean read · he won't notice you noticed

choices.label: wave Faith over · order coffee · let her run the table read.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · Faith works the booth · diner's own threshold tightens

choices.label: read him the way the_girl_at_the_back_of_the_parish was read.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · pattern-match against a known threshold reader · requires the back-porch thread

stages.title: the booth · facing him
stages.body:
Booth 6 is where {agent} sits now. Across the formica from him. He doesn't look up · the ticket wheel turns once · the fluorescent ticks. The threshold the diner maintains has a soft spot in it and the soft spot is between this booth and the back hallway.

What {agent} brings to the table decides whether the soft spot closes.

choices.label: two cups of coffee · don't say his name · let the booth do the work.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the booth's own threshold closes · clean exit

choices.label: ask him the one question he can't answer here.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · he leaves · the booth holds · -1 cover (the next table heard the question)

effects.reason: the question carried to the next table

choices.label: do nothing · wait him out · let the diner's regulars close the table.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · slowest · safest · costs nothing now

resolution_flavor_success: Booth 6 was empty by close. The wear-spot was the wear-spot again.
He left at 3:48 PM · cup half-drunk · ticket wheel turned once on its own. The diner held.
resolution_flavor_failure: {agent} read the booth wrong. Booth 6 was still occupied at close · Faith wiped around him like he wasn't there.

templates.title: Surveillance Posture
templates.flavor: Cameras at the gate. License plate readers at the entrance. The HOA's new security committee is meeting every Tuesday. The posture is the thing — the cameras are the symptom.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The cul-de-sac is now a surveilled space. Future operations there cost +50%.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Eyes that already know the road. Someone who can read camera angles without being read by them. This is a multi-stage mission — the survey, the committee meeting, the posture move.

stages.title: walking the gate · counting the lenses
stages.body:
{agent} walked the south gate at 7:48 AM. Six cameras visible · three are recent (matte-black, new bracket mounts) and three were already here (cream-colored, sun-faded). The plate reader is a 1996 Genetec model · the same one Mobile installed last fall.

The survey takes the form the survey takes.

choices.label: count + map · build the kit-list.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · clean info · no flags raised

choices.label: follow one of the cameras' cable run back to its source.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · finds the head-end · 1 cover (you were on someone's tape for that walk)

effects.reason: agent on tape walking the cable run

stages.title: the Tuesday security-committee meeting
stages.body:
The HOA's new security committee meets Tuesday at 7 PM in the clubhouse. Three voting members + Mrs Salinas as the secretary (she takes minutes; the minutes go in the records desk by Wednesday). Tonight: the camera-installation budget for August.

How {agent} reads the room determines what gets in the minutes.

choices.label: sit in the back · listen, don't comment.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · the minutes go in as filed · no cover risk

choices.label: raise the Genetec system's '95 false-flag rate.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · plants doubt in the committee · costs 1 cover · marker: hc_surveillance_escalated for 10 days

effects.reason: agent on the HOA security-committee meeting roster

effects.log_line: [color=#c8a8ff]Marker set:[/color] surveillance-escalated (10 days). The committee is watching harder · quiet-week events in HC won't fire until the heat drops.
effects.expiry_line: [color=#a8c0a8]The HC security committee's heat has dropped. The neighborhood breathes again.[/color]

choices.label: talk to Mrs Salinas after · the minutes are flexible.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the minutes go in soft · requires having read the W6 HC kitchen thread

templates.title: HOA Action
templates.flavor: A specific ordinance, a specific fine, a specific notice nailed to a specific door. The HOA does this in writing and the writing is legible later.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Someone who reads ordinances for fun. The HOA's writing is the kind of writing that wins on its own terms — match it. This is a multi-stage mission — read the notice, find the precedent, file the response.

stages.title: the notice on the door
stages.body:
{agent} pulled the notice off the door at 9:42 AM. Three paragraphs, Tahoma 11, double-spaced. The ordinance cited is Section 4.7 of the bylaws. The fine is $150 if unanswered in seven days.

The notice has a specific person's name typed at the bottom · either the HOA secretary or one of the committee members. Reading whose name it is tells you what was meant.

choices.label: read the bylaws cited section.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · finds the technical defect · no cost

choices.label: call the name at the bottom directly.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · resolves with conversation · 1 cover (your name is on their list now)

effects.reason: phone call to the HOA notice signer

stages.title: the response
stages.body: By the end of the day {agent} has a response prepared. The form of the response matters as much as the content. The HOA reads what they like to read.

choices.label: match their format · 3 paragraphs Tahoma 11.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · clean response in their language

choices.label: cite a different ordinance · push back.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · sets up a longer fight · the file goes on the chairman's desk by next Tuesday

effects.reason: the chairman reads pushback as defiance; the next notice will be specific

choices.label: write the response in Mackenzie's own voice.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · disarms the HOA · requires having read her unleashed-on-the-boards thread

templates.title: Missing Kid
templates.flavor: Not yet a national story. Yet. The flier is at the community pool. The mother is in the HOA office at 9 AM Monday and at 9 AM Tuesday and at 9 AM Wednesday.

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.text: It went national. Local coverage is now national coverage. Cover in the region is gone.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The first 48 hours is a canvass. The second 48 is whether a lead lands. This is a two-stage mission and the clock is the clock.

stages.title: the first 48 hours · door · pool · mother
stages.body:
{agent} started at the community pool at 7:14 AM Tuesday. The flier is on the bulletin · the corners are curled · the photo is from his school spring photos (blue background · the haircut is six months old). Eleven doors knocked between the pool and the cul-de-sac. The mother is at the HOA office at 9. The deputy is at the office at 11.

The canvass takes the shape the canvass takes.

choices.label: the canvass · door · pool · bus stop. Quiet · methodical.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · clean intel · slow · no flags raised

choices.label: go to the mother first · sit at her table · let her talk.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · she tells you the thing the deputy didn't write down · -1 cover (you are now in her phone)

effects.reason: agent now in the mother's contact list

choices.label: tap the south parish wire · ask if anyone moved the kid through.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · network ask · requires reading the MS_006 thread

stages.title: the second 48 hours · the lead
stages.body:
A lead came in. It's the kind of lead that's either right or wasn't a lead at all. The deputy is parked at the HOA office and the news van is parked at the pool. The mother is at the office at 9 again.

Which way {agent} runs the lead changes whether the deputy gets there first or the mother does.

choices.label: give the lead to the deputy · let the badge work it.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the deputy moves · public · -1 cover · risks press if it doesn't land

effects.reason: agent on record handing off to the deputy

choices.label: run the lead privately · find him yourself · hand him back to the mother.
choices.summary: +2.0 effort · costs the agent's day · the mother gets the kid back · no press · -2 sanity

choices.label: hand the lead to filly · she works it through her network.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · cleanest · she has the contacts · she will owe nothing back

resolution_flavor_success: He came home Thursday afternoon. The deputy filed the report and the report stayed local.
The flier came off the pool bulletin Friday morning. Faith pinned a thank-you card up in its place.
resolution_flavor_failure: The flier is still on the bulletin. The corners are more curled. The mother is at the HOA office at 9 AM again on Thursday.

templates.title: Community Meeting Goes Wrong
templates.flavor: The Thursday-night HOA meeting. Or the Wednesday-night town hall in Small Wood. The motion was supposed to be the motion. The motion is now something else.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: A meeting is a room. The room is rigged the day before. This is a two-stage mission · prep the room · then read it.

stages.title: the agenda · the night before
stages.body:
{agent} got eyes on the printed agenda at 8:42 PM Wednesday. The motion the chair plans to take to vote is item 4 of 7. Item 5 is the budget line that pays for item 4. Item 6 is what someone added late · it's the one that's now the meeting.

The night before is when the room gets shaped.

choices.label: call three voting members · count the room · don't lobby.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · accurate count · no debt incurred

choices.label: lobby moth or mackenzie · get them to chair the discussion.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the chair moves · costs a favor

choices.label: submit a counter-motion · file it before the agenda locks at 10 PM.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · changes item 6 to your item 6 · -1 cover (your name is on the filing)

effects.reason: agent on record as motion filer

stages.title: the room · the night of
stages.body:
The room is 24 chairs · two long tables · the chair's gavel that hasn't been used since '94. Seven voting members at the front · twelve residents in the chairs · two reporters from the local in the back row.

What {agent} does in the room shapes the minutes Mrs Salinas types Friday.

choices.label: sit in the back · don't speak · count hands.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · pure intel · the room votes as it votes

choices.label: speak during open comment · use the 1994 inspection roster.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the room shifts · -1 cover · requires having read the library thread

effects.reason: agent on the public record at open comment

choices.label: talk to the reporters after · steer the writeup, not the vote.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the vote stands · the writeup is yours

resolution_flavor_success: The meeting adjourned at 9:34 PM. The motion that needed to die did. Mrs Salinas's minutes are the agent's minutes.
The chair gavelled it down on a 4-3 vote. Two reporters left without a story.
resolution_flavor_failure: The motion passed. Mrs Salinas typed the minutes Friday and the minutes are exactly what passed. Item 4 of 7 is now policy.

templates.title: Newsletter Item
templates.flavor: Page three of the HOA newsletter, the column the residents read first. The item is named in a way that lets adjacent residents know who it's about.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The newsletter is words on paper. Match them with words on paper, or get the words off the paper before it prints. Pick the angle. This is a short two-stage mission · the print deadline is Thursday.

stages.title: the draft on the editor's desk
stages.body:
{agent} got eyes on the draft at 2:18 PM Tuesday. The column on page 3 is 240 words. The item names a 'recent resident' and the adjacent paragraph names the cul-de-sac. The two together don't quite name anyone but they read close enough.

Thursday is the print deadline.

choices.label: ask the editor (Patricia) for a sentence-level edit.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · clean editorial fix · the item still runs but unnamed

choices.label: submit a competing item · push it off page 3.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the column gets bumped · costs no cover

choices.label: give Patricia the Mackenzie 'how-to-write-without-naming' note.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · disarms the whole column for the season · requires reading Mackenzie's W8 letter

templates.title: Model Home Has The Wrong Feel
templates.flavor: The model home is, this Tuesday, slightly cold around the kitchen island. The realtor walking the next family through notices and doesn't, on her way out, say what she noticed.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The model home is a stage set · the cold around the kitchen island is the audience leaving. Walk the room · find what's wrong · close it. Two stages.

stages.title: the walkthrough · finding the cold
stages.body:
{agent} let themselves in at 10:18 AM Wednesday. The lockbox code was 4429. The kitchen island is two degrees colder than the rest of the kitchen · the realtor's clipboard is on the counter with a sticky note that says 'Tuesday family — pass.' The light over the sink is on a dimmer that was set yesterday and hasn't been touched since.

What {agent} reads first changes what they find.

choices.label: walk the rooms in the order the realtor walks them · front door · living · kitchen · master.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · sees what the realtor sees · the cold registers

choices.label: walk the rooms backwards · master · kitchen · living · entry.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · finds the source · the cold tracks back to the kitchen island

choices.label: stand at the kitchen island and just listen · don't move for ten minutes.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · finds what the silence is · -1 sanity (it talks to you)

stages.title: the close · changing the room
stages.body:
The kitchen island is two degrees colder and that two degrees is the wrong audience leaving the show. The realtor is back at 1 PM Friday with the Walsh family. The fix can be done at 11 AM Friday and not before · the room has to be empty enough to hold what {agent} puts in it.

Three ways to close the cold.

choices.label: reframe · move the fruit bowl · change the magazines on the coffee table · re-stage.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the realtor's eye reads the room as new · the cold is hidden, not gone

choices.label: smudge the room · sage · cedar · the white candle in the master closet.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · costs nothing visible · clears the kitchen island for ~14 days

choices.label: have cicada walk the room with the realtor at 1 PM.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · cicada softens the room itself · the family signs Friday

resolution_flavor_success: The Walsh family signed on Friday at 2:14 PM. The realtor wrote a thank-you note that mentioned 'a warm kitchen.'
The realtor walked through Friday and didn't notice the kitchen island. Which is what was supposed to happen.
resolution_flavor_failure: The Walsh family walked out before the master bedroom. The realtor didn't ask why.

templates.title: Local Press Exposure
templates.flavor: Page B6 of the Small Wood Gazette. Five paragraphs, three of them about Frasier's contact's brother. The fourth is about the room above the timber yard.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Press is paper · paper has a deadline. Read the piece · decide whether to kill it, redirect it, or own the next one. Two stages.

stages.title: the clipping · what's actually on B6
stages.body:
{agent} pulled the Wednesday Gazette at 6:18 AM. B6 is below the fold · five paragraphs · the byline is Roy Castellan (he's been at the Gazette since '91 and writes mostly farm-loan stuff). Paragraphs 1-3 are the contact's brother (Reggie Tighe · arrested in March · plea this fall). Paragraph 4 mentions 'a rented room above the timber yard' without naming Jules.

What {agent} does with the clipping decides what next Wednesday's B6 looks like.

choices.label: read it carefully · file it · don't engage Roy.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · pure intel · the piece runs its course

choices.label: call Roy · ask about his sources · light pressure.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · Roy now knows someone's reading · -1 cover (he writes it down in his notebook)

effects.reason: agent now in Roy's reporter notebook

choices.label: feed elicia a competing item · the cathedral fundraiser angle.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the next B6 is a softer story · elicia owes a future favor back

stages.title: the room above the timber yard · the source
stages.body:
Paragraph 4 came from somewhere. The room above Jules's timber yard is rented (the door is at the top of the back staircase; the key is with Jules). Roy didn't name it because he doesn't have the name yet. Someone walked him to the corner of it.

Three ways to close the source.

choices.label: ask Jules quietly · who's been on the staircase.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · Jules names them or doesn't · clean

choices.label: move the room · clear it Friday · the room above the yard is now the room above the feed store.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · physical move · -1 sanity · the next piece has nothing to point at

choices.label: pull MS_006 thread context · ask the south-parish regulars who's been talking.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · network ask · finds the leak · requires the MS_006 thread

resolution_flavor_success: Next Wednesday's B6 is the cathedral fundraiser. Roy's notebook stayed in his desk.
The leak was an HOA receptionist who'd been to the yard once in May. She didn't know what she was repeating.
resolution_flavor_failure: The Wednesday after, B6 named the room. Jules locked the staircase door from the outside and stopped renting the upstairs for the rest of the year.

templates.title: Contact Going Dark
templates.flavor: The call back hasn't come. It's Tuesday. By Friday it's a thing.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: A contact gone dark is a person who decided. Find the why before the door · two stages, the second one only if the first one says go.

stages.title: the soft check · phone · pager · second number
stages.body:
{agent} pulled the file at 8:14 AM Tuesday. Three numbers · the home line (no answer, no voicemail picking up · the bungalow phone has been down on and off since W4), the pager (one buzz · no reply), the second number (the one only the agent has).

Which number {agent} works changes whether the contact knows they're being checked on.

choices.label: the home line · the answering machine · leave a normal message.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · low pressure · the contact may or may not call back · no cost

choices.label: the second number · ring twice and hang up · the pre-arranged signal.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the contact knows you're asking · -1 sanity (you waited by the phone)

choices.label: call the bungalow back-channel · ask if anyone's seen them.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · network ask · requires reading MS_004 (bungalow phone-outage thread)

stages.title: the door · if the check came back wrong
stages.body:
The check came back the way it came back. {agent} is now at the contact's door · the porch is the porch · the screen is the screen. The light inside is on a timer that hasn't been changed in two years.

What {agent} brings to the door determines what's behind it.

choices.label: knock · the agent's normal knock · two-and-one.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the contact recognizes the knock or doesn't · safe · slow

choices.label: walk around back · the alley · check the kitchen window.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · find out if it's an absence or a decision · -1 cover (the neighbor watched)

effects.reason: neighbor watched the agent in the alley

choices.label: send john_frank · he knew them in '88 · the door opens for him.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the door opens · costs john_frank a day · cleanest

resolution_flavor_success: She called back Wednesday at 11:18 AM. She'd been at her sister's in Pensacola. The file is back open.
He answered the door for John Frank. The two of them sat on the porch until 4 PM. The contact stays in the file.
resolution_flavor_failure: No one picked up. By Friday the file moved to inactive. The room above the timber yard has a new key now.

templates.title: Seed Dying
templates.flavor: A planting attempt that hasn't taken. The room is rented and quiet but no one is going to it. The contact is on the way out. The next plant is going to cost more.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: A planting that hasn't taken is a planting that has to be reviewed before it gets reburied. Two stages: walk the room, then decide whether to nurse it, abandon it, or replant.

stages.title: the room · walk through · diagnostic
stages.body:
{agent} let themselves into the rented room above the yard at 9:14 AM. The bed is unmade · the lamp is on (it's been on since Sunday) · the contact's keys are on the kitchenette counter (which means they've been back at some point) but the milk in the fridge is from the W6 dairy run.

The walkthrough tells {agent} which kind of dying.

choices.label: walk every room · open every drawer · build the picture.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · full diagnostic · the agent now carries the contact's whole shape · -1 sanity

choices.label: leave a note · the agent's normal note · walk out · come back tomorrow.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · soft · gives the contact one more chance · clean

choices.label: call jules to come up with you · two pairs of eyes.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · jules sees what the agent misses · costs jules a half-day

choices.label: bring the_surviving_son · run the '94 transition protocol · sit one afternoon before deciding.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · uses the back-porch method for room-transitions · the room re-shapes cleanly · requires BP_018

stages.title: nurse · abandon · replant
stages.body: The room is read. The contact's last sign-on was Sunday. The contact is on the way out — that's not a maybe. Three paths and they all cost something.

choices.label: nurse · the_surviving_son sits with the contact for 4 days · he can do this.
choices.summary: +2.0 effort · costs the_surviving_son 4 days · the planting takes

choices.label: abandon · close the room · the contact gets a clean exit · the next plant is harder.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · cheap now · expensive later · -1 future plant difficulty

choices.label: replant · move the contact to the room above the feed store · fresh start.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the contact gets a different door · -1 cover (the move is on someone's tape)

effects.reason: the move was on a neighbor's eye

resolution_flavor_success: The contact made the sign-on Thursday at 7:14 AM. The lamp was off when {agent} walked past Friday.
The_surviving_son sat across from him every morning for four days. By the fifth morning the contact made his own coffee.
resolution_flavor_failure: The room was empty Friday. The keys were still on the counter. {agent} closed the door behind them and gave Jules the key back.

templates.title: Demon Corruption Event
templates.flavor: A demon currently in Small Wood is teetering. The corruption rating is at threshold. The next dispatch decides whether you pull her or push her.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: A demon teetering is a choice the agent will not make twice in a year. Two stages: the read (what kind of corruption), the call (pull her, push her, or hold the door). Filly is the only handler who can do this clean.

stages.title: the read · what kind of corruption
stages.body:
{agent} found her at 4:38 AM Thursday on the dirt road north of Frasier's plant. She's not the demon she was Sunday. Her hands are different · the way she stands is different · she speaks in the wrong tense.

What kind of corruption decides what kind of choice. {agent} has to read it before deciding.

choices.label: ask her what day it is · listen to the tense she answers in.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · diagnostic · safe · clean read

choices.label: show her the photograph from the file · the one of the seed-room.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · she breaks toward the photo or away from it · either is a read

choices.label: call filly's pre-arranged frequency · she's already heard the news.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · filly carries the read · clean · filly is on the road within the hour

stages.title: the call · pull her, push her, or hold the door
stages.body:
The read is in. Three paths. Pull her — bring her back to the safehouse and start the long un-binding (costs days and a handler). Push her — speed the corruption along on purpose so she becomes the resistance's, not Frasier's (costs a contact). Hold the door — leave her on the road · let her choose.

The call is the call. {agent} carries it the rest of the year.

choices.label: pull her · safehouse · filly works the un-binding for 6 days.
choices.summary: +2.0 effort · clean · filly is offline for the week · costs 6 days of filly capacity · marker: filly_at_safehouse for 6 days · unlocks Death · the_quiet_committal

effects.log_line: [color=#c8a8ff]Marker set:[/color] filly at the safehouse (6 days). No corruption-adjacent events in Small Wood while she's off the road.
effects.expiry_line: [color=#a8c0a8]Filly's un-binding closed at day %d. She's back on the road tomorrow.[/color]

choices.label: push her · speed it along · she comes back on resistance side.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · she returns · -1 contact (the contact who was running her support drops you) · she is now ours

choices.label: hold the door · stand back · let her choose.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · the lowest effort and the highest cost · the outcome is the outcome

resolution_flavor_success: She's at the safehouse. Filly closed the door at 11 PM and the un-binding starts Monday.
She chose. The road was empty by 6 AM. Filly drove back to town in the rain.
resolution_flavor_failure: The corruption took. She's not ours and she's not Frasier's anymore either. The dirt road north of the plant is unsafe until further notice.

templates.title: The Ground Refuses
templates.flavor: Sometimes Small Wood does not want what Frasier is sending. The dirt road that was supposed to be a node says — through the locals who walk it — no.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The ground says no through people · listen to which people. Single stage · the choice is how you negotiate with the refusal.

stages.title: the dirt road · the locals who walk it · what they say
stages.body:
{agent} walked the dirt road at 6:48 AM. It's the road that used to lead to the Tibideaux place; it's been a footpath since '74. Three locals walk it: Mr Beauchamp at 5:30, the Lacombe sisters at 7 (rosaries · they pray a different decade on each leg), and the boy who walks his dog past the bend at 5 PM.

None of them spoke to {agent} but each of them changed their pace at the same spot · twenty feet past the bend, the road thins. The ground there is refusing the node.

choices.label: listen to the locals · drop the plan · pick a different node site.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the ground was right · the new site takes · clean

choices.label: negotiate · talk to the Lacombe sisters at the chapel after their walk.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · they tell {agent} what the ground is holding · -1 sanity (they take a piece of you back)

choices.label: push through · the node goes in anyway · the ground takes longer to refuse.
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the node opens but the road's locals stop walking it · -2 cover (the absence is noticed)

effects.reason: the road went quiet; absence is its own signal

resolution_flavor_success: The new site is the new site. The Lacombe sisters walked the same decade on both legs the next morning.
Mr Beauchamp went back to his normal pace at 5:30. The road accepted what got planted.
resolution_flavor_failure: The road has been empty at 7 AM for nine days. Jules drives past it on his lunch and doesn't slow down.

templates.title: Town Meeting Pushback
templates.flavor: The Wednesday town hall in Small Wood. The room knows who's new. The room has been the room for fifty years.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The Small Wood room has been the room for fifty years. The room does not respect lobbying · the room respects history. Single stage · the choice is who speaks for you.

stages.title: the room · fifty years of itself
stages.body:
{agent} stood at the door of the Small Wood town hall at 6:48 PM Wednesday. Forty folding chairs · the same forty chairs since '74. The mayor is Earl Boudreau (third term) · the deputy mayor is his cousin · the secretary is the cousin's wife. The pushback on the agenda item is from the back row · seven faces · all known.

The room is the room. {agent}'s play has to fit the room.

choices.label: have jules speak · he's been here forty years · the room listens.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · clean · the room nods · jules carries the day · costs jules a day of capacity

choices.label: {agent} speaks themselves · short · invokes the parish history.
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the room hears a new voice · -1 cover · could go either way

effects.reason: agent on the open-comment record in Small Wood

choices.label: skip the room · go to Earl's house Thursday morning instead.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · private · Earl is the room · costs an inflexible favor he'll cash one day

resolution_flavor_success: The motion passed 5-2. The back row left without saying anything to each other on the way out.
Earl shook Jules's hand at the door. They didn't speak of the meeting again.
resolution_flavor_failure: The motion was tabled. The back row stayed in their seats for ten minutes after adjournment, talking quietly.

templates.title: Turned Demon Active
templates.flavor: A demon Frasier trained is now on the resistance's side. The problems originate from a name on the map that used to be one of his.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: A turned demon is an asset and a liability at once. Husk is the only one who can keep her functional. Two stages: integrate her into the network, then decide her assignment.

stages.title: the integration · husk works the seams
stages.body:
Husk took her in at the safehouse off the back road at 11:14 PM Sunday. She's not the demon Frasier trained anymore but she's also not a clean asset — Frasier's training is still in her hands, still in the way she reads a room. The work is teaching her which reflexes to keep and which to retire.

The integration is the integration. How {agent} structures it decides how stable she comes out.

choices.label: husk does it alone · two weeks · she comes out clean.
choices.summary: +2.0 effort · husk offline 14 days · clean integration · cleanest outcome

choices.label: husk + filly · faster · filly cross-trains so this is repeatable.
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · 8 days · filly learns the method · -1 sanity for filly

choices.label: shortcut · field-integrate · she goes on the next dispatch.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · risky · she keeps some of Frasier's reflexes · -2 future stability

stages.title: the assignment · what she does for us now
stages.body: She's integrated · in whatever shape the integration left her. Now the question is what she's for. Three roles to choose from · each uses her differently · each makes her differently visible.

choices.label: embed her in small_wood · she walks the dirt roads · the ground learns her.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · slow build · she becomes part of the region · +1 small_wood agent capacity over time

choices.label: send her into Frasier's territory · she knows his methods · risky.
choices.summary: +2.0 effort · high-value intel · -2 cover · -1 sanity for husk (he watches her go)

effects.reason: she's back on Frasier's side · this time as ours

choices.label: she rests · the assignment is herself · she lives in the safehouse and we let her become.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · no immediate value · clean · she may surface later in some form we don't know yet

resolution_flavor_success: She walked the dirt road north of the plant Wednesday morning. Mr Beauchamp passed her and tipped his hat.
Husk closed the safehouse door at sundown. She was inside · she was ours · the work would hold.
resolution_flavor_failure: The integration didn't hold. She left the safehouse Thursday at 2 AM. Husk drove the back roads for three days and didn't find her.

templates.title: Wake Problem
templates.flavor: Spawned by Steamboat's failure mode. The wake registered three counties over and the wake is now its own problem.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The wake is the wake · noise · light · weather. Two stages: source the wake, then dampen it (or carry the dampening costs).

stages.title: the source · where the wake came from
stages.body:
{agent} pulled the levee gauge at 5:14 AM. The river is up six inches since Tuesday · two of those inches are Steamboat's. The wake registered at the dock in Mobile and Mrs Hadley's wind-chimes went off in Pensacola at 4:11 AM. Steamboat does not yet know the wake registered.

How {agent} sources the wake decides whether Steamboat finds out.

choices.label: ride the levee · count the gauges · note the time on each.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · clean source data · Steamboat does not know

choices.label: tell Steamboat directly · the wake registered · here are the gauges.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · he tightens his own behavior · -1 sanity (he carries it)

choices.label: cross-check against the levee thread · ask the regulars what they saw.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · finds two more gauges than the official record · requires reading MS_00B_LEVEE

stages.title: the dampening · close the wake
stages.body:
The wake is the wake and now it has to close. Weir is on the dock at 8 AM · Moth is in the field at noon · the Mobile harbor-master is on the line at 1 PM asking if anyone wants to file what they saw.

Three ways to dampen it · each is its own cost.

choices.label: weir takes the dock · standard dampening · the harbor-master gets a polite no-comment.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the wake closes · weir is offline for one day

choices.label: moth carries the dampening · the wake closes through the trees, not the river.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the wake closes in the wrong direction · -1 sanity for moth · slow burn

choices.label: let the harbor-master file it · own the paper trail · clean public response.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the wake closes officially · -1 cover · -1 future cover-recovery

effects.reason: the wake is on the harbor-master's paper trail

resolution_flavor_success: The river dropped four inches by Saturday morning. The harbor-master did not call back.
Weir stood on the dock until the wake closed. He did not speak to Steamboat about it.
resolution_flavor_failure: The wake did not close. By Sunday it had registered in three more counties. Steamboat called the agent at 11 PM and the agent did not pick up.

templates.title: Cathedral Basement Relay
templates.flavor: The W14 storm came north of the keys. The cathedral basement is the inland relay for the gulf-coast circle. JF is on the boiler; the line stays up only as long as someone's at the desk. Cross-BBS coordination through the storm window.

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.text: [color=#ff9090]The relay dropped at 11:48 PM. STEEPLE tried to call back from Mobile and got no carrier.[/color]

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.reason: the relay dropped; the gulf-coast circle holds it against you

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The storm window is the storm window. JF holds the boiler · someone holds the desk · someone holds the line up the coast. Three stages, one per role · this is the W14 hard branch and it stays in the file.

stages.title: the boiler · JF · the noise that holds the line
stages.body:
{agent} got to the cathedral basement at 7:14 PM Friday. The boiler is on its winter cycle even though it's August · JF said it has to be. The pilot is the pilot · the relief valve is the relief valve. JF is forty feet from the desk and he can hear the line by the way the boiler hums.

How the boiler is run during the storm decides what the line sounds like.

choices.label: JF runs the boiler · agent stays out of his way.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · JF carries it · costs JF 2 days of capacity post-storm

choices.label: agent works the boiler alongside JF · learns the rhythm.
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the agent picks up the boiler skill for future storms · -1 sanity

choices.label: swap steamboat in · he knows the gulf-coast cycle · JF takes the desk.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · steamboat on boiler · cleanest under pressure · -1 cover (steamboat is now on the basement schedule)

effects.reason: steamboat on the cathedral basement schedule

stages.title: the desk · the line stays up
stages.body:
The desk is a folding table, a CB radio (modified for the relay frequency), a notebook, and three pens of different colors. Whoever's at the desk reads the carrier into the notebook every fifteen minutes. STEEPLE comes through at 9 PM, 11 PM, 1 AM, 3 AM, 5 AM. The desk stays manned until 6:14 AM Saturday.

Who holds the desk shapes what the gulf-coast circle remembers about this storm.

choices.label: agent holds the desk · all night · five carrier reads · no swap.
choices.summary: +2.0 effort · clean · -2 sanity · the cleanest log

choices.label: two-shift rotation · agent + weir · weir takes 1 AM to 5 AM.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · clean enough · weir spent one night · agent stays functional Saturday

choices.label: tap the MS_010 storm-watch readers · ask one of the regulars to sit the desk.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · pulls a civilian into the relay · -2 cover · cleanest log next-day · requires the storm-watch thread

effects.reason: civilian pulled into the basement relay

stages.title: the coast · the call south
stages.body:
5:48 AM Saturday. The storm is north of the keys but still inland. STEEPLE is on the line from Mobile. The gulf-coast circle wants the all-clear or the hold. Whoever picks up the basement carrier carries the call south.

This is the last stage of the relay. The choice carries through W15 and beyond.

choices.label: all-clear · we held the relay · gulf circle continues normal.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · clean exit · the circle is intact

choices.label: hold · we held but the line is thin · gulf circle goes quiet for 72 hours.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · safer · costs 3 days of gulf-coast inbound traffic

choices.label: escalate · the relay is degraded · ask STEEPLE to route through Pensacola next storm.
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the circle reshuffles · -1 cover (you're on the circle's notes now) · the cathedral isn't the relay next W14

effects.reason: agent named on the gulf-coast routing notes

resolution_flavor_success: The relay held. STEEPLE logged the carrier through at 5:48 AM. The boiler cooled by Sunday morning.
JF sat on the cathedral steps Saturday at 6:30 AM smoking a cigarette he hadn't smoked in three years. The line had stayed up.
resolution_flavor_failure: The relay dropped at 11:48 PM. STEEPLE tried to call back from Mobile and got no carrier. The gulf-coast circle holds it against you for the rest of the year.
